title: Add "podman volume" command
body:
Add support for podman volume and its subcommands.
The commands supported are:
	podman volume create
	podman volume inspect
	podman volume ls
	podman volume rm
	podman volume prune

This is a tool to manage volumes used by podman. For now it only handle
named volumes, but eventually it will handle all volumes used by podman.

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body:
volume driver support is still WIP and working on adding the tests.
This allows users to create volumes using the podman volume tool, or users can create named volumes directly when doing a podman run or create. (e.g `podman run -v foo:/foo alpine sh`) and it will create foo, under /var/lib/containers/storage/volume.
Eventually, I believe all the volumes should be created under /var/lib/containers/storage/volume. That means if we do `podman run /path/to/data/ondisk:/path/in/container alpine`, we will copy the contents from `/path/to/data/ondisk to /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/random_vol_name` and mount that into the container. This would allow users to easily manage all their volumes in one place.

@mheon could you please double check that I didn't miss anything in the DB stuff.
/approve

@umohnani8 We should never copy the "bind" mount volumes into local storage.  Users expect the Bind mount volumes to share the content with the outside world. 

I disagree with your statement https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1904#issuecomment-443195631
One thing you need to make sure of is that named volumes you create have the SELinux label container_file_t on them.
Awesome job, now got to fix the failing test above.
minor nit in commit description, "cubcomands" to "subcommands"

README.md needs updating.

Also will need to add these API endpoints to varlink to allow varlink to manage volumes.

Question: do we ever intend on supporting volume drivers? I think the way Docker handles them is really poorly suited to our daemonless workflow...
@rhatdan @umohnani8 thoughts?
@mheon I would like to, I am not sure why daemonless would be a problem, but discuss on IRC if you want.
Forgot to mention, I'm assuming tests are inbound?

@rhatdan not sure what in README.md needs to be updated, but I updated commands.md.
@TomSweeneyRedHat yeah will add tests in a bit, sort of moved on to work on other things for a bit :)
Added SELinux label container_file_t to the named volumes. Added tests.
Ok lets get this to pass tests and then get this merged,  Urvashi leaves for PTO this week, so we can get it in and have others fix issues that we find when we and QE plays with it.
/test images
